channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: The $1.8 billion pivot: How Michael Bloomberg’s historic gift rewrote the rules of college access
item.description: Michael Bloomberg's $1.8 billion donation to Johns Hopkins University in 2018 revolutionised higher education by funding need-blind admissions. This historic gift eliminated financial barriers for talented students, allowing them to pursue education based on merit, not wealth. The endowment ensures a lasting legacy of opportunity, empowering future generations to excel and contribute to society without the burden of overwhelming debt.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: The $22 million mystery: Why a secret donor handed a small Canadian hospital the gift of a lifetime
item.description: A remarkable $22 million donation to Yarmouth Hospital Foundation in Nova Scotia has transformed local healthcare. The anonymous donor's gift, intended for perpetual funding through investments, ensures over $1 million annually for patient benefits. This unprecedented act of generosity shifts focus from immediate needs to long-term planning, offering a powerful example of quiet philanthropy's lasting impact on regional communities.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Kash Patel grilled over allegations of 'excessive drinking': 'If you want to pop bottles...'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Watch: Gunshots heard at Philippine Senate, lawmakers run for cover
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Jashanpreet Singh jailed for over 5 years in California after police seized illegal weapons
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: The Dragon and the Dealmaker: Tariff warrior Trump arrives in Beijing as a trade pilgrim
item.dc:creator: Chidanand Rajghatta

item.title: Bernard Arnault’s simple billionaire business lesson that most companies forget under pressure
item.description: Bernard Arnault, CEO of LVMH, champions a long-term vision for business success. He advises against fixating on quarterly results, stressing that patience and quality build lasting brand value. This approach fosters trust and customer loyalty, crucial for enduring appeal. Companies prioritizing short-term gains risk damaging their reputation and future. Arnault's philosophy offers a sustainable path to outperforming peers financially.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: The one Google rule, 'focus on the user', that helped build a trillion-dollar company
item.description: Google's meteoric rise is attributed to its core belief: 'Focus on the user and all else will follow.' This simple yet powerful principle guided product design, prioritizing user satisfaction over immediate business gains. By minimizing friction and enhancing interactions, Google fostered loyalty and drove growth, proving that user-centricity is a potent business strategy.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: UK police recover bodies of three women from sea off Brighton beach
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: ‘She’s luckier than me’: UK woman wins £333,000 lottery prize after husband secretly put her name
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Saudi Arabia launched secret retaliatory airstrikes on Iran during regional war: Report
item.description: Saudi Arabia conducted unpublicized airstrikes on Iran in late March, marking the first direct military action on Iranian soil in retaliation for attacks within the kingdom. This shift in approach followed Iranian strikes on Gulf Cooperation Council states, leading to an informal de-escalation understanding between Riyadh and Tehran.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Hegseth pushes Trump’s $1.5 trillion defense budget for 2027 as Congress presses Pentagon over Iran war costs
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth defended President Trump's proposed $1.5 trillion military budget amid congressional scrutiny over the Iran war's escalating costs, now at $29 billion. Lawmakers questioned the strain on weapons stockpiles, while Hegseth acknowledged increased production to support ongoing operations and future needs.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Who is Maya Higa? Twitch streamer makes history with first-ever TED Talk and standing ovation
item.description: Maya Higa made history by becoming the first Twitch streamer to deliver an official TED Talk, earning a standing ovation from the audience in Vancouver. During the speech, she spoke about using livestreaming to inspire animal conservation through her Alveus Animal Sanctuary. Maya revealed her community has raised over $7.5 million for conservation efforts since 2019. Fans and fellow creators praised the emotional moment online, calling it a major milestone for Twitch and digital creators.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: What happened between Trevv and ChudTheBuilder? Nashville confrontation turns into aggravated robbery controversy
item.description: ChudTheBuilder and Trevv’s online feud exploded after Trevv stole ChudTheBuilder’s hat during a public Nashville encounter on May 10, 2026. The situation quickly turned serious after accusations of aggravated robbery, weapon claims, and FBI comments surfaced online. Despite multiple viral posts and livestream reactions from both creators, no arrests or confirmed investigations have been publicly reported so far.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: Row over Pete Davidson's 'dark humour' on Charlie Kirk: 'He constantly makes 9/11 jokes...'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Missing Indian man Sandarsh Krishna found dead in Grand Canyon, no cause of death established yet
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: D4vd appears relaxed in court during murder case status hearing in Los Angeles
item.description: Singer D4vd, David Burke, appeared in a Los Angeles courtroom for a status hearing in his murder case. Charged in connection with the death of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez, Burke pleaded not guilty. Lawyers discussed evidence exchange and case timelines, with the next hearing set for June 17 and the preliminary hearing on June 29.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy
